title: Suki's Starlight Adventure
synopsis: When little Suki's favorite star disappears from the night sky, she feels scared and sad. With the help of her grandmother and magical fireflies, she embarks on a gentle adventure to find it, discovering that sometimes what we're looking for is closer than we think.

pages.text: Every night, Suki looked out her bedroom window to say goodnight to her special star. It was the brightest one in the sky, and it always made her feel happy and safe. But tonight, something was wrong. Her star was gone! Suki felt scared and sad.

illustration.description: A young Japanese-American girl with short black hair in purple pajamas standing at a bedroom window, looking worried. The night sky is dark blue with many small stars, but there's an obvious empty space where the brightest star should be. Her room has warm yellow lighting from a small lamp, creating a cozy but concerned atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Suki looking sadly out her window at the night sky

pages.text: "Grandma Mei!" called Suki, running to the kitchen where her grandmother was making tea. "My star is missing! I can't say goodnight without it!" Grandma Mei, with her kind eyes and silver hair, sat down next to Suki. "Don't worry, little one," she said gently. "Sometimes stars need our help to shine bright again."

illustration.description: Suki hugging her elderly Asian grandmother in a warm kitchen with yellow walls. Grandma Mei has silver hair in a bun and wears a soft blue cardigan. Steam rises from two teacups on the wooden table. Both characters have caring, gentle expressions in the cozy, lamp-lit room.
illustration.alt: Suki seeking comfort from her wise grandmother in the kitchen

pages.text: Grandma Mei took Suki's hand and led her outside to the garden. "Look," she whispered, pointing to the bushes. Tiny fireflies began to glow like little golden stars! "The fireflies will help us find your star," said Grandma. Suki felt a little bit brave now. She followed the dancing lights deeper into the garden.

illustration.description: Suki and Grandma Mei in a magical garden at night, surrounded by glowing fireflies that create warm golden dots of light. The garden has purple and green plants, and both characters look amazed and hopeful. The fireflies form a gentle path leading toward some tall sunflowers in the background.
illustration.alt: Suki and Grandma following magical fireflies through the garden

pages.text: The fireflies led them to the big sunflower patch. There, caught gently in the tall flower petals, was something shimmering and bright. "Your star fell down to visit the flowers!" laughed Grandma Mei. Suki felt so happy! She carefully helped the star float back up to the sky, where it belonged. "Thank you for coming to see our garden," she whispered to her star.

illustration.description: A magical scene showing Suki reaching up toward a glowing, friendly cartoon star nestled among tall yellow sunflowers. The star has a sweet smiling face and sparkles with silver and gold light. Grandma Mei watches proudly as fireflies dance around them in the dark blue night.
illustration.alt: Suki discovering her star among the sunflowers

pages.text: Back in her bedroom, Suki looked out the window and smiled. Her special star was twinkling brightly in the sky again. "Goodnight, star. Goodnight, fireflies. Goodnight, Grandma," she said sleepily. As Grandma Mei tucked her in, Suki felt safe and loved. She had been brave, and now her star would always shine for her.

illustration.description: Suki peacefully sleeping in her bed with a content smile, while Grandma Mei gently pulls up her colorful quilt. Through the window, the bright star twinkles happily in the dark sky, and a few fireflies can still be seen glowing softly in the garden below.
illustration.alt: Suki sleeping peacefully with her star shining brightly outside

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Suki feel when her star was missing?
questions.explanation: Suki felt scared and sad when she couldn't find her special star in the sky.

questions.question: Who helped Suki find her star?
questions.explanation: Grandma Mei helped Suki, and the magical fireflies led them to where the star was hiding.

questions.question: Where was Suki's star hiding?
questions.explanation: The star had fallen down and was caught gently in the tall sunflower petals.

questions.question: How did Suki feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: At the end, Suki felt safe and loved, knowing her star was back in the sky and her grandma was taking care of her.

pages.title: Complete the Sentence
pages.text: Suki looked out her window to say _____ to her special star.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Word
pages.text: The tiny _____ began to glow like little golden stars!

pages.title: Complete Suki's Adventure
pages.text: Suki's star was hiding in the _____ patch.
